name: book-production-roadmap
description: 'Turn a reviewed manuscript into a release plan. Use for structure freeze, editorial sequencing, figure program, notebook integrity, citations, external readers, and release-candidate criteria.'
argument-hint: 'State the book title, current manuscript maturity, and whether major correctness issues are already closed.'
user-invocable: true
---

# Book Production Roadmap

Use this skill when the manuscript is no longer in raw-draft mode and needs a forward-looking plan for becoming the best publishable version of the book.

## When to Use

- major correctness issues are mostly closed;
- the next question is no longer "what is wrong?" but "how do we ship this well?";
- the repo needs a phased production plan instead of a defect backlog; and
- you need explicit exit criteria for each pass.

## Procedure

1. Read the current manuscript structure and build configuration.
2. Separate unresolved trust issues from production tasks.
3. Write a north star and a definition of done.
4. Build a phased roadmap covering structure, editorial pass, figure work, notebooks, citations, output polish, external readers, and release candidate.
5. Keep the plan tied to the actual repo rather than generic publishing advice.

## Rules

- Do not pretend a repo is in production phase if correctness is still materially open.
- Prefer finite phases with exit criteria.
- Keep the reader and artifact target explicit.
- Replace stale review backlogs with a production roadmap only when the repo is ready for that shift.

## Recommended Template

- [Production roadmap template](./assets/production-roadmap-template.md)
